title: Met issues yellow warning of heat wave, thunderstorms in parts of Himachal Pradesh - ThePrint
search_term: heatwave (Bilaspur OR Chamba OR Hamirpur OR Kangra OR Kinnaur OR Kullu OR "Lahaul and Spiti" OR Mandi OR Shimla OR "Sirmaur (Sirmour)" OR Solan OR Una) Himachal Pradesh
full_text:
Shimla, May 19 (PTI) A yellow warning of a heat wave in Kangra and Solan for Wednesday has been issued by the meteorological office in Shimla on Tuesday, officials said.
It also issued a yellow alert for thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds (speed 40-50 kmph) at isolated places in Chamba, Kangra and Kullu for Thursday and Friday, and Mandi and Shimla for Friday, they said.
The weather remained mainly dry in the state in the past 24 hours since Monday evening, except for Bhuntar, which received 19 mm of rain and Manali, which received traces of rain, they added.
Kukumseri, recording a low of 6.1 degrees Celsius, was the coldest at night, and Una, with 43.4 degrees Celsius, was the hottest during the day. A yellow alert of a heat wave in Solan for Wednesday and Thursday has been issued, they said.
Mercury also breached the 40-degree mark in Neri, which recorded a temperature of 41.2 degrees Celsius. PTI BPL SHS SHS
